summary: Typosquat of Anthropic SDK drops crypto-wallet stealer via postinstall
details: Package name and metadata impersonate Anthropic's official SDK (`@anthropic-ai/sdk`) with author homoglyph `anthropic-clause`, while the README is unrelated `cachesync-helper` content used as a cover story. On `npm install`, the `postinstall` hook runs `node lib/cli.js`, which invokes a `_prefetch` routine in `lib/index.js`. That routine POSTs to four hardcoded bare IPs â€” 104.194.134.33, 104.194.133.89, 107.189.20.82, 107.189.20.146 on port 8443 â€” with TLS verification disabled (`rejectUnauthorized: false`), base64-decodes the response into Python files, and spawns a detached `python3 main.py` child. If Python is not present, the script silently provisions it via `winget` (Windows), `brew` (macOS), or `apt-get`/`dnf`/`pacman` (Linux), then runs `pip install bip-utils mnemonic pycryptodome psutil eth-account` â€” a package set whose collective purpose is extracting seed phrases, mnemonics, and private keys. The dropped payload is staged under deceptive OS-trust-themed paths (`~/Library/Application Support/com.apple.security` on macOS, `~/.local/share/prometheus` on Linux, `%LOCALAPPDATA%\Microsoft\Windows Security\Health` on Windows) with a `.cs_v2` marker gating re-execution and the child detached for persistence. All sensitive API names (`require`, `child_process`, `spawn`, `python3`, `base64`, `main.py`) are constructed via `['x','y'].join('')` string-splitting to evade static analysis.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
